title: Complex Numbers
summary: Complex number arithmetic, modulus, argument, polar form, De Moivre's theorem, and complex roots.
content_html:
Definition

A complex number z = a + bi, where a,b ∈ ℝ and i = √(-1), so i² = -1.

  • Real part: Re(z) = a
  • Imaginary part: Im(z) = b

Arithmetic

Addition:       (a+bi) + (c+di) = (a+c) + (b+d)i
Subtraction:    (a+bi) - (c+di) = (a-c) + (b-d)i
Multiplication: (a+bi)(c+di) = (ac-bd) + (ad+bc)i  [using i²=-1]
Division:       (a+bi)/(c+di) = (a+bi)(c-di)/[(c+di)(c-di)]
                               = (ac+bd)/(c²+d²) + (bc-ad)/(c²+d²)·i

Complex Conjugate

z̄ = a − bi (negate imaginary part). Key property: z·z̄ = a² + b² = |z|²

Modulus (Absolute Value)

|z| = √(a² + b²)

Argument

arg(z) = θ = arctan(b/a)  (adjust for correct quadrant)

Polar Form

z = r(cos θ + i sin θ) = re^{iθ}    where r = |z|, θ = arg(z)

Euler's formula: e^{iθ} = cos θ + i sin θ

Multiplication in Polar Form

z₁z₂ = r₁r₂ · e^{i(θ₁+θ₂)}   (multiply moduli, add arguments)
z₁/z₂ = (r₁/r₂) · e^{i(θ₁-θ₂)} (divide moduli, subtract arguments)

De Moivre's Theorem

zⁿ = rⁿ(cos nθ + i sin nθ) = rⁿe^{inθ}

nth Roots of a Complex Number

The n roots of z = re^{iθ} are:

wₖ = r^{1/n} · exp[i(θ + 2πk)/n]  for k = 0, 1, ..., n-1

Example: Cube Roots of 8

z = 8 = 8e^{i·0}
w₀ = 2, w₁ = 2e^{i·2π/3} = 2(-1/2 + i√3/2) = -1+i√3
w₂ = 2e^{i·4π/3} = -1-i√3

exercises.title: Polar multiplication
exercises.prompt: In polar form, multiplying two complex numbers:

exercises.title: Number of nth roots
exercises.prompt: How many nth roots does a non-zero complex number have?
